items.title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts and rumors
items.summary: NFL free agency opened with the legal negotiating window at noon ET Monday. We're following all the moves.

items.title: Sources: Commanders, CB Robertson reach deal
items.summary: The Commanders have reached a two-year, $16 million deal with former Lions cornerback Amik Robertson that includes $9.3 million guaranteed, according to NFL Network.

items.title: Falcons reach deal with TE Hooper, sources say
items.summary: The Atlanta Falcons and tight end Austin Hooper have agreed to a one-year, $3.25 million contract, according to NFL Network.

items.title: Colts re-sign WR Pierce, trade Pittman to Steelers
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts are re-signing wide receiver Alec Pierce but are trading his running mate Michael Pittman Jr. out of town.

items.title: TE Charlie Kolar to sign three-year deal with Chargers
items.summary: The Chargers are signing tight end Charlie Kolar to a three-year, $24.3 million contract, NFL Media reports.

items.title: Report: Colts signing Titans free agent edge Arden Key to 2-year, up to $20M deal
items.summary: According to NFL Insider Jordan Schultz, the Indianapolis Colts are signing Tennessee Titans free agent edge Arden Key to a 2-year, $20 million deal (with $11 million guaranteed): Originally a 2018 3rd round pick of the Raiders franchise, the 6,’5”, 240 pound edge rusher is coming off a season for the Titans defense in which […]

items.title: Colts add DE Arden Key
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts continue to address their defensive front. The team has agreed to terms with former Tennessee Titans defensive end Arden Key on a two-year, $20 million contract with $11 million in guarantees, according to a source. NFL Network was first to report the agreement. Key, 29, has had at least 4 [...]

items.title: Colts reportedly sign free agent pass rusher from division rival
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts are reportedly signing a free agent pass rusher from one of their division rivals.

items.title: Dolphins take $99m hit on Tagovailoa and sign Willis; Tampa’s star WR Evans heads to 49ers
items.summary: The Miami Dolphins are moving on from Tua Tagovailoa, the quarterback they drafted with the fifth overall pick in 2020 in hopes of turning the franchise’s fortunes around.  “As we move forward, we will be focused on infusing competition across the roster and establishing a strong foundation for this team as we work towards building a sustained winner,” Dolphins general manager Jon-Eric Sullivan said in a statement on Monday.  The move will cost the Dolphins an NFL record $99m in dead money against the salary cap.

items.title: Alec Pierce has always been a 'priority' for the Colts. This is one big reason why.
items.summary: Pierce's ability to win on vertical, downfield routes provides a skillset that's becoming harder and harder to find in today's modern NFL.

items.title: Report: Bears poach Colts’ latest backup free agent defensive lineman
items.summary: According to NFL Network’s Tom Pelissero, the Chicago Bears have quickly signed Indianapolis Colts free agent defensive tackle Neville Gallimore to a 2-year, $12 million deal during the legal tampering period: It comes exactly one offseason after the Bears also poached former Colts backup versatile defensive lineman Dayo Odeyingbo to a 3-year, $48 million deal […]

items.title: Report: Colts WR Michael Pittman Jr. traded to Steelers
items.summary: Indianapolis, IN — The Indianapolis Colts establish their wide receiver room moving forward after trading longtime wideout Michael Pittman Jr. to the Pittsburgh Steelers, according to NFL Insider Tom Pelissero. The compensation includes a late-round pick swap, as this move more so points to Indy’s lack of cap space to include Pittman Jr. in their […]

items.title: Colts trading Michael Pittman Jr. to Steelers
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS -- Long-time Indianapolis Colts wide receiver Michael Pittman Jr. is heading out east. The Colts are trading Pittman to the Pittsburgh Steelers, according to ESPN's Adam Schefter. Schefter reported the deal includes a swap of late-round picks plus a contract extension for 3 years and $59 million from Pittsburgh. The 2020 second-round pick ranks [...]

items.title: The Alec Pierce Sweepstakes Ends in Historic Fashion
items.summary: Indianapolis, IN — The Indianapolis Colts made history today by signing wide receiver Alec Pierce to the biggest free agent contract his position has ever seen, a 4-year, $114m deal containing $84m in guarantees. Pierce is fresh off a season in which he lit the league on fire. Securing his first 1,000-yard season (1,003 yards) […]

items.title: Rapid Reaction: Pierce secures record deal to sign with Colts
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts made a massive commitment to retain one one of their best skill players, signing standout receiver Alec Pierce to a four-year, $116 million contract extension. The deal was negotiated by agents Mike Swenson and CJ LaBoy and was agreed on before Monday. The contract carries an average annual value of $29 million, […]

items.title: Report: Colts and FA WR Alec Pierce agree to 4-year, $116M contract extension—keeping him in Indianapolis
items.summary: According to ESPN’s Pat McAfee of ‘The Pat McAfee Show’ (via ESPN’s Adam Schefter), the Indianapolis Colts and highly coveted, otherwise soon-to-be free agent wideout Alec Pierce have agreed to a 4-year, $116 million contract extension, right around where the league’s official tampering period would’ve otherwise opened at noon today (EST): It’ll be interesting to […]

items.title: Colts WR Alec Pierce announces new contract with Colts

items.title: Colts, WR Alec Pierce reach long-term deal
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Alec Pierce isn’t going anywhere. Pierce agreed to a 4-year, $116 million deal, according to multiple sources. Word first came via "The Pat McAfee Show." Pierce, who turns 26 in May, was expected to be one of the most coveted free agents when the NFL’s negotiating window opened at noon Monday. The deal [...]

items.summary: Grupe made all 11 of his field goals, including a franchise-record 60-yarder, over five games with the Colts in 2025.

items.title: Indianapolis Colts free agent WR replacement options if Alec Pierce departs soon

items.title: Colts, NFL prepare to deal for free agents
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The NFL’s version of "Let’s Make a Deal" is almost upon us. Even though the new league year begins Wednesday at 4 p.m., free agency gets a head start at noon Monday. That’s when the Indianapolis Colts and 31 other teams are allowed to approach players with expiring contracts. Deals can be arranged [...]

items.title: Colts reportedly offered FA QB Daniel Jones ‘Sam Darnold money,’ but his camp had rather lofty counter offer
items.summary: According to SI.com’s Albert Breer, the Indianapolis Colts offered pending free agent starting quarterback Daniel Jones ‘Sam Darnold money’, but Jones and his camp countered with a surprising $50 million per year contract proposal: “The Colts’ initial offer to Jones was in the range of Sam Darnold’s three-year, $100.5 million deal last offseason,” writes Breer. […]

items.title: Colts re-sign K Blake Grupe
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — The Indianapolis Colts re-signed Blake Grupe, according to a source, giving them a quality backup or training camp competition at kicker. Grupe converted all 21 of his kicking attempts last season — 11 field goals, 10 PATs — as the Colts were forced to utilize three kickers for the first time in franchise [...]

items.title: Colts trade linebacker Zaire Franklin to Packers for defensive tackle Colby Wooden
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS — The Indianapolis Colts addressed their defensive front with some addition and subtraction. The team has traded linebacker Zaire Frankin to the Green Bay Packers for defensive tackle Colby Wooden. The move isn’t official until the start of the new league year Wednesday. The Colts began exploring trade possibilities for Franklin, their long-time leading [...]

items.title: Ryan Kelly retires after 10 seasons
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Ten seasons were enough. Ryan Kelly, a 2016 first-round draft pick and anchor of the Indianapolis Colts for nine seasons, announced his retirement on Friday on his social media account. “What an incredible ride it was,’’ he posted on his X account. Kelly finished his career with the Minnesota Vikings in 2025, but [...]

items.title: Source: Colts exploring trade for Zaire Franklin
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – One of the Indianapolis Colts’ defensive cornerstones might be on the move. The team is exploring trade possibilities for linebacker Zaire Franklin, a source confirmed. ESPN was first to report the situation. The Colts entered the offseason needing to address their salary cap and trading Franklin would clear $5.755 million in cap space [...]

items.title: Colts' Alec Pierce: I've earned the right to explore free agency
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Alec Pierce could be excused for experiencing flashbacks. You know, to when he was a multi-sport standout at Glenbard West High School in Glen Ellyn, Ill. and his speed and deep-threat skills in football were drawing the attention of D-I schools. It was when he had all the leverage. Which college is best [...]

items.title: Colts using transition tag on Daniel Jones
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Tag. Daniel Jones is it. The Indianapolis Colts’ commitment to moving into 2026 with Jones as their starting quarterback consists of applying the seldom-used transition tag, according to a source with knowledge of the situation. It’s a guaranteed, one-year $37.833 million deal. However, it comes with risk. The transition tag allows Jones to [...]

items.title: Colts place transition tag on QB Daniel Jones
items.summary: The transition tag gives the Colts the right to match any offer Jones receives in free agency.
